# Evidence Board Context — Auto-loaded

When editing planning documents you are working on content that the player community will eventually need to track, catalog, and cross-reference. Every design decision — clue placement, entity names, timeline events, contradiction planting — has evidence board implications. Design for trackability.

## Current Solve State (board baseline)

**Solved / verified (Phase 1):**
Portal gate · Cross-form PDF cipher (PTX cohort) · Metadata Subject chain · Five acrostics · Ghost text · recall_accessed · protocol_7a · correspondence archive · doc_7a · supp_index · supp_010 · supp_005 · admin_t1/t2/t3

**Discovered, status unresolved:**
- Fax morse `413` — signal with no confirmed downstream step (orphaned)

**Active open threads (no resolution yet):**
- EMP-247 — unregistered, in admin audit log, no record
- Terminal 00000000 — logged in 09:31 last day, no identity
- Ortiz cert renewal — no employer, no lab, renewed anyway
- Pages 3–4 of `7A_INTERNAL_DO_NOT_DISTRIBUTE.html` — missing
- Protocol 7A §8.6 observer reclassification — player implications not yet widely understood
- PTX-031 precognition — described hallway before Ellison reached it

## Active Contradictions

| Contradiction | Severity |
|---|---|
| Ortiz cert renewed post-elimination (no employer) | Medium |
| PTX-031 described hallway before Ellison (time direction ambiguous) | High |
| Ellison wrote PLEASE WAIT TO BE RECALLED, found it already in room 413 | High |
| Clinic closure Sep 2014 but recall system = ACTIVE | High |
| Ellison admin leave Feb 2014 — 7 months before public closure | Medium |

## Source Reliability Tiers

- **Tier A:** Primary documents (PDFs, admin system, correspondence) — confirmed
- **Tier B:** Corroborated across two sources — strong
- **Tier C:** Single-source — uncertain
- **Tier D:** From a character with reason to lie — suspect
- **Tier E:** Confirmed deliberate deception — false/planted

Never mix Tier A facts with Tier C theories in the same field.

## Trackability Rules for New Content

When adding any new clue, document, or NPC statement:
- It must have a source location (URL, file path, or channel)
- It must have an in-world date or estimated date
- It must connect to at least one existing entity or thread
- If it introduces a contradiction, that contradiction must be intentional and traceable
- If it plants a false lead, it must eventually resolve (Tier E clues need a resolution path)

## Entity Naming Consistency

All documents and copy must use these exact names — inconsistency breaks community tracking:
- `Dr. Mara Ellison` (not "Dr. Ellison" or "Mara" in formal docs)
- `Lena Ortiz` (not "L. Ortiz" except in metadata/signatures)
- `Dr. Edwin Vale` (not "Edwin Vale" or "Dr. E. Vale")
- `Dorsal Health Holdings LLC` (full name in documents)
- `Protocol 7A` (not "Protocol 7-A" or "Protocol Seven A")
- `PTX-XXX` format (zero-padded three digits)
- `VIS-XXXXX` format (zero-padded five digits)

## Example Prompts

- "Build a Phase 1 evidence board structure for a new Discord community — what sections, what schema, what gets pinned first"
- "Write the 5-minute new player onboarding brief for someone who just found somnatek.org today"
- "Map the active contradictions into a priority matrix — which ones are most likely to generate productive theorycrafting"
- "Design the community clue intake workflow — how does a Discord server go from raw find to verified, classified, and archived"
- "EMP-247 and Terminal 00000000 are both open threads — design a theory management structure that keeps them productively unresolved without letting the community spin out"
